rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BRAVO VS. TULARE DISTRICT HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Transfer Action To Proper Court And For Costs And Attorney'S Fees [Ccp 394, 395, 396b(B)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 25, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT TULARE DISTRICT HOSPITAL Motion To Transfer Action to Proper Court and For Costs and Attorney's Fees [CCP 394, 395, 396b(B)]. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EPISCOPAL SENIOR COMMUNITIES F/K/A EPISCOPAL HOMES VS. WACHOVIA BANK, N.A., A U.S. CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 25, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT WACHOVIA BANK, N.A., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment, or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant produced no evidence allowing an inference that plaintiff neither possesses, nor reasonably can obtain, evidence to support its causes of action. Furthermore, a triable issue of material fact is raised whether proper notice was given with respect to the termination date. Exhibit G to the Maher declaration provides a phone number, not a fax number. Triable issue of fact whether instructions were provided from ESC to fax correspondence to Ms. Luchese and/or to fax at any particular number. No evidence that ESC in any way interfered with defendant's notice attempts. Triable issue of fact whether defendant complied with notice requirement when timely telephonic notice not provided. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL HORAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 25, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, amended Complaint filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE LEE VS. STEVE LY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 25, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT STEVE LY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled for uncertainty; however, demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend to add Lu as a party. CCP section 389(a).

rulings.Case Title: JAMES NICHOLSON et al VS. BARBARA GREGORATOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Deft'S Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice Of Deft'S Anti-Slapp
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 25, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANTS BARBARA GREGORATOS, and, JEFFREY BRANDSTETTER Notice Of Defendant's Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Complaint. Motion denied, defendants fail to show that the causes of action arise from protected activity. While the service of a notice to terminate may be privileged, the service was not the cause of plaintiffs' causes of action. The gravamen of plaintiffs' complaint is the defendants' failure to comply with the provisions of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance. See Marlin vs. Amico Venezia LLC (2007) 154 Cal.App. 4th 154 and Clark vs. Mazgani (2009) 170 Cal.App. 4th 1281. Insufficient evidence presented that defendants' motion was frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. Request for attorney fees denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. JACK D SCHLOTTHAUER et al
